message:
perf(core): inline NULL/Integer/Float SUM accumulation to eliminate per-row dynamic dispatch (bd-wwqen.4)

Replace the generic aggregate function registry lookup and virtual step()
calls in the COUNT(*)+SUM fast path with direct match-arm accumulation:

- Integer values accumulate via checked_add, promoting to float on overflow
- Float values accumulate directly into a separate f64 accumulator
- Null values are skipped without touching the accumulator
- Text/Blob triggers a rare fallback path that re-scans through the
  registered sum aggregate, preserving correct coercion semantics

This avoids the overhead of borrowing func_registry, looking up "sum/1",
allocating initial_state(), and dispatching through the trait-object step()
on every single row — all of which dominated the hot loop for numeric-only
tables (the overwhelmingly common case).

The test import reorder in fast_path_separation.rs is a rustfmt
normalization (alphabetical ordering of use items).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
